question_text: Two identical sealed containers of gas are prepared. Container A is kept at room temperature, and Container B is heated to a much higher temperature. Both containers have the same volume and the same amount of gas. Compare the pressure in both containers and explain your answer using the particle model.
correct_answer: Container B has higher pressure than Container A. When gas is heated, the particles gain kinetic energy and move faster. Faster-moving particles collide with the container walls more frequently and with greater force, so the pressure increases. Since both containers have the same volume and amount of gas, the only difference is the particle speed caused by temperature.
explanation: This question requires students to understand the relationship between temperature and particle movement, and how this translates to pressure changes. It goes beyond simple recall by asking them to compare two scenarios and explain the mechanism using particle theory.
